clusters.title: Prima KottuMee Spices Up New Zealand Under‑85kg Tour in Sri Lanka

versions.title: Prima KottuMee Spices Up New Zealand Under‑85kg Tour in Sri Lanka
versions.deck: Prima KottuMee returns as the Hot ’N’ Spicy food partner for the second year running, supporting Sri Lankan rugby as it gears up for the New Zealand Under-85kg Tour 2026 — a testament to the brand’s steadfast commitment to sports.

clusters.title: Symbolic governance and the cost of silence

versions.title: Symbolic governance and the cost of silence
versions.deck: A bank does not lose billions overnight. It loses it in instalments - through unchallenged assumptions, unasked or unanswered questions, missed warning signals and the quiet comfort of believing that someone else is watching the controls. It is this timely but uncomfortable truth that the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey captured with clinical precision, speaking at the CII Corporate Governance Summit in

clusters.title: The financial fraud at NDB Bank

versions.title: The financial fraud at NDB Bank
versions.deck: The financial fraud of a staggering amount of Rs. 13.2 billion at NDB Bank is without precedent in Sri Lanka. It has sent shock waves throughout the industry and among investors. The possibility of recovery of the stolen funds seems to be very uncertain at this point of time. The NDB Board collectively have to take the ultimate responsibility for this serious financial fraud. They have been in receipt of lavish amounts paid to them as emoluments

clusters.title: From terrorist iconography to political iconography: Who owns the Easter Sunday narrative?

versions.title: From terrorist iconography to political iconography: Who owns the Easter Sunday narrative?
versions.deck: Seven years after the 2019 Sri Lanka Easter Sunday attacks, Sri Lanka continues to live not only with the grief of that day, but also with the unresolved struggle over how the tragedy is remembered, interpreted, and politically used. Over 260 innocent people were killed, hundreds were injured, and thousands of families were left with pain that time has not erased. Yet each year, as the commemoration returns, so too does a familiar contest over na

clusters.title: Five years have passed, but he’s still remembered

versions.title: Five years have passed, but he’s still remembered
versions.deck: Mangala Samaraweera passed away in 2021. Had he lived, he would be 70 on 21 April. What is the measure of a politician? What people say at the funeral and in eulogies that only highlight positives? It seems that what would matter more is what they’d say years later. Would they still remember? Would there be anything to talk about five or ten years later? Not having conducted a survey, I only have anecdotal evidence about Mangala. On Monday, I

clusters.title: Roadmap targets tripling insurance penetration by 2035 with mandatory cover, State-backed schemes

versions.title: Roadmap targets tripling insurance penetration by 2035 with mandatory cover, State-backed schemes
versions.deck: The regulator and insurance industry have unveiled a national roadmap aimed at doubling insurance penetration by 2030 and tripling it by 2035, including proposals to introduce mandatory home insurance and Government-supported schemes to widen coverage. The Insurance Regulatory Commission of Sri Lanka (IRCSL), in partnership with the Insurance Association of Sri Lanka (IASL), yesterday launched the Insurance Industry Development Roadmap.

clusters.title: Trinity leads charge in redefining Schools’ Rugby ’26

versions.title: Trinity leads charge in redefining Schools’ Rugby ’26
versions.deck: Prelude Following a swashbuckling and captivating performance by Trinity College Kandy, that raised the bar of schools’ rugby standards above that of Sri Lanka rugby, the platform is now set to renovate, innovate and elevate all forms of rugby played in the nation to the next level. clusters.title: Motor insurance to shift towards cash-before-cover from 1 May

versions.title: Motor insurance to shift towards cash-before-cover from 1 May
versions.deck: Insurance Association of Sri Lanka President and HNB Assurance PLC Chief Executive Officer Lasitha Wimalaratne yesterday said the industry will tighten credit practices in motor insurance, with a phased shift towards a cash-before-cover model from 1 May.

clusters.title: Rs.9 b Hayleys Rights issue oversubscribed; attracts Rs. 11.05 b subscriptions

versions.title: Rs.9 b Hayleys Rights issue oversubscribed; attracts Rs. 11.05 b subscriptions
versions.deck: The Hayleys PLC yesterday said its recently announced Rights issue has been oversubscribed, drawing total subscriptions of over Rs. 11 billion. The company had sought to raise Rs. 9 billion via a rights issue of 45 million ordinary voting shares at Rs. 200 per share, as announced on 21 January 2026.

clusters.title: Tea output dips 14.5% in March amid dry weather

versions.title: Tea output dips 14.5% in March amid dry weather
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s tea production fell sharply in March, hit by prolonged dry and hot weather conditions, according to data analysed by Asia Siyaka Commodities PLC. Output declined to 20.8 million kilograms (Mn/Kgs), down from 24.4 Mn/Kgs registered in March last year, a drop of 3.5 Mn/Kgs or 14.5% year-on-year (YoY).

clusters.title: Fuel distributors seek President’s intervention over revised commission structure

versions.title: Fuel distributors seek President’s intervention over revised commission structure
versions.deck: The Petroleum Distributors’ Association has appealed to President Anura Kumara Dissanayake, calling for urgent intervention to revise the current fuel dealer discount mechanism, warning that the existing system threatens the viability of filling stations across the country.

clusters.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, succeeded by John Ternus

versions.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, succeeded by John Ternus
versions.deck: Apple has announced that Tim Cook will become Executive Chairman of Apple’s board of directors and John Ternus, senior vice president of Hardware Engineering, will become Apple’s next CEO effective on 1 September 2026. The transition, which was approved unanimously by the Board of Directors, follows a thoughtful, long-term succession planning process, the company said in a statement.

clusters.title: PDMO gains global recognition with prestigious Commonwealth Award

versions.title: PDMO gains global recognition with prestigious Commonwealth Award
versions.deck: Sri Lanka has achieved a landmark milestone in its public debt management reform journey, with the Public Debt Management Office (PDMO) being awarded “Debt Management Office of the Year (Asia-Pacific)” at the Commonwealth Debt Management Award Ceremony held at Marlborough House, London, on 26 March, in parallel with the Commonwealth Debt Management Forum held from 23–27 March.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka pushes to centre unpaid care work in economic policy

versions.title: Sri Lanka pushes to centre unpaid care work in economic policy
versions.deck: By Charumini de Silva At a time when South Asia is rethinking labour markets and social protection, Prime Minister Dr. Harini Amarasuriya yesterday called for a “fundamental rethinking” of how economies define productivity, urging policymakers to recognise unpaid care work as a core pillar of economic systems.

clusters.title: What ails our sports bodies?

versions.title: What ails our sports bodies?
versions.deck: With every passing year, Sri Lankan sports recede further into the shadows, an also-ran competitor, in many sports not even qualifying to compete at top international events. The one exception being the game of Cricket, a game played mainly among former British dominions and colonies. Today the mainstay of Cricket are the South Asian countries, which provide much of the money and a vociferous fan base for the game. The game of Cricket itself has

clusters.title: Abans Finance gets CSE nod for Rs. 1.5 b debt issue

versions.title: Abans Finance gets CSE nod for Rs. 1.5 b debt issue
versions.deck: The Colombo Stock Exchange yesterday said it has granted in-principle approval for the listing of proposed debt securities of Abans Finance PLC. The proposed issue comprises an initial tranche of 10 million listed, rated, senior, unsecured, redeemable five-year (2026/2031) debentures at a face value of Rs. 100 each. The company has the option to issue a further 5 million debentures in the event of an oversubscription, taking the total issue size

clusters.title: Conspiracy against the Central Bank independence: Don’t kill the golden goose

versions.title: Conspiracy against the Central Bank independence: Don’t kill the golden goose
versions.deck: The topic of Central Bank Independence (CBI) has been the subject of extensive discussion in various forums, including Parliament and the news media in Sri Lanka, over the years. The long-awaited goal of CBI was finally achieved with the enactment of the Central Bank Act in 2023, the biggest landmark in the history of monetary policymaking. clusters.title: Uphill task for Galle after Dambulla gain 233-run lead

versions.title: Uphill task for Galle after Dambulla gain 233-run lead
versions.deck: By Sa’adi Thawfeeq Defending champions Galle faced an uphill task of saving their National Super League 4-day match against last year’s runner-up Dambulla at the R Premadasa Cricket Stadium yesterday after conceding a huge first innings lead of 233 runs. clusters.title: Shirantha outlines his vision for NOCSL if he wins Presidency

versions.title: Shirantha outlines his vision for NOCSL if he wins Presidency
versions.deck: By Shamseer Jaleel Current Vice President Shirantha Peries on Monday outlined his vision to contest the National Olympic Committee of Sri Lanka (NOCSL) presidency, promising a new era of transparency, unity, and stronger governance for Sri Lankan sports. clusters.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka

versions.title: New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team to tour Sri Lanka
versions.deck: The Rangiri Dambulla Cricket Stadium will be the venue for New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ team’s six-match tour of Sri Lanka next month. The New Zealand Women’s ‘A’ side is due to arrive in Sri Lanka on 7 May and commence their tour with a 3-match WODI series on 12 May against Sri Lanka Women’s ‘A’ side. They will also play a 3-match WT20I series commencing 21 May. All matches will be day games commencing at 10 am. TOUR SCHEDULE 12 May: 1st One-Day

versions.title: Ceylon Masters International Badminton Tournament: 25–29 June
versions.deck: Withdrawal penalty: Withdrawals after the draw, carries a penalty of Rs. 5,000. Failure to pay within 14 days of the tournament completion will result in a 12-month ban from CMB sanctioned tournaments. No refunds after the draw closes.

versions.deck: Sri Lanka Esports is all set to conduct the Esports Nationals, the official national team selection qualifiers conducted by the Sri Lanka Esports Association (SLESA), as the country begins its journey towards major international competitions starting with the Asian Games 2026 in Japan, where Esports will be featured as a medal event. versions.deck: In a world where sport is no longer confined to just performance on the court, but extends into lifestyles, community, and digital experiences, HUTCH steps into partnering with Sri Lanka Padel League (SLPL) 2026, fuelling connectivity, energy, and a shared passion for rising sport. versions.title: BETSS.COM powers Sri Lanka’s horse racing with landmark three-year sponsorship
versions.deck: BETSS.COM, the digital platform of Sporting Star, is ushering Sri Lanka’s horse racing into a new era through a landmark three-year title sponsorship of the BetSS Governor’s Cup and BetSS Queen’s Cup. clusters.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch

versions.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch
versions.deck: Fitch Ratings has said that Sri Lanka’s recovery remains fragile despite improved macroeconomic fundamentals, with the Middle East energy shock now testing the gains achieved through stabilisation and reforms. In its latest Sri Lanka update on credit development, Fitch said growth is expected to slow, inflation to rise and the fiscal deficit to widen in 2026, even as the economy builds on stronger fundamentals following the 2022 crisis. versions.title: Sri Lanka’s bumpy road to a political reset
versions.deck: By International Crisis Group Promises of “system change” As it nears completion of eighteen months in power, the National People’s Power (NPP) Government of President Anura Kumara Dissanayake has kept Sri Lanka’s fragile economic recovery on track but is struggling to live up to bold promises of “system change”. versions.title: Benedictines retain Luke Shield for fifth consecutive year
versions.deck: St. Benedict’s College maintained its stranglehold on the prestigious Luke Shield, beating Wesley College by four wickets at the Singhalese Sports Club grounds on Saturday, 11 April 2026. This limited-overs encounter between two of the oldest Christian schools in the country has been vigorously fought over since its inception in 2022, and the Benedictines have won each time. clusters.title: Ajith De Silva elected President of Mercantile Hockey Association

versions.title: Ajith De Silva elected President of Mercantile Hockey Association
versions.deck: Ajith De Silva was elected President of the Mercantile Hockey Association (MHA) at its 70th Annual General Meeting held on 28 March 2026 at the Burgher Recreation Club.

clusters.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock

versions.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s economic growth is expected to slow to 3.6% in 2026 as higher energy prices weigh on activity, although the economy will regain its pre-crisis size last seen in 2018, the World Bank said in its latest South Asia Economic Update April 2026.
